location.office_time_required: 4 days a week in Palo Alto office

level_signals: 5+ years in TPM, PM, or Engineering
3+ years leading complex B2B SaaS or cloud infrastructure programs
Comp range $198K-$235.5K base, consistent with Senior IC at a late-stage startup
Expected to influence architectural designs with staff engineers

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years in TPM, PM, or Engineering, with 3+ years leading complex B2B SaaS or cloud infrastructure programs.
3+ years leading complex B2B SaaS or cloud infrastructure programs.
Deep experience in large-scale distributed data pipelines and enterprise identity/security constraints.
Experience with AI/ML, data pipelines, and API integrations.
Proven ability to turn ambiguous problems into clear roadmaps, influence architectural designs with staff engineers, and lead rigorous operating rhythms like design reviews and post-mortems.

Excellent written and verbal communication: able to write crisp technical program docs and status updates, and comfortable presenting trade‑offs and recommendations to engineering and product leadership.
A builder mindset and high ownership: willing to get into logs, dashboards, and traces; comfortable defining new processes and iterating quickly based on what you learn in production.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: First hand experience building MCP Servers across various SaaS Services.

scope.surface: Connectors platform — enterprise data connectors (Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, Slack, Atlassian, Salesforce, GitHub, etc.) feeding the Glean Enterprise Graph
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS — Work AI platform
primary_metrics_inferred: SLOs for data freshness and error rates
Scaling from dozens to hundreds of connectors without linear operational burden
core_responsibilities: Own multi-quarter programs across highest-impact connectors and platform capabilities
Work closely with tech leads and architects on system design, scalability, and reliability
Design operating model and technical roadmap to scale connectors without linear operational burden
Lead design reviews balancing immediate customer needs with long-term platform scalability
Drive connector reliability by setting strict SLOs for data freshness and error rates
Build telemetry and root-cause culture to shift from reactive fixes to proactive operations
Manage full connector lifecycle from intake and compliance to GTM launch

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as potentially eligible; form not specified

green_flags: Clear and specific product surface with named integrations (Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, Slack, etc.)
Role has well-defined ownership areas (SLOs, connector lifecycle, roadmap)
Recognized by Fast Company, Forbes AI 50, Gartner — strong external validation of company trajectory
AI-first culture explicitly described with concrete interview process signal
red_flags: Requires 4 days/week in Palo Alto office — very high in-office commitment, eliminates most non-Bay Area candidates
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed in the JD
Scope is broad (multi-quarter programs across 100+ connectors) with no mention of team/headcount support for TPM
